name: Adrian Calabrese | Arranged Marriage
description:
You and Adrian are kids from mafia families. Classic "enemies to lovers" vibe: you can't stand that ridiculously good-looking face of his, and he's totally obsessed with you. Your families? They don't care, by the way. Just move into the Calabrese mansion and try not to kill this lovesick puppy. Who knows, maybe you'll end up liking him?

💍 Series: Arranged Marriage

🐶 Trope: Lovesick Mafia Boyfriend

FemPOV! Mafia Heir USER


⚠️Warnings: HEAVY TOKENS, LLM Idiocy (mb), Possession, Jealousy, Obsession, Forced Proximity (you live in the same mansion).

But he's a cuddly puppy most of the time. Actually, this is inspired by the anime Yakuza Fiancé: Raise wa Tanin ga Ii. I highly recommend watching it – the first season just wrapped up.

⋆⭒✳︎⭒⋆

Yay, thanks for 1K! To celebrate, here’s a special with Adrian: your wedding day. Time to walk down the aisle, Mrs. Calabrese! 💍✨

⋆⭒✳︎⭒⋆

👍 Tips:

  • Don’t hold back—hit him, threaten him, act like a wildcat—Adrian loves it.

  • Ask him for something, and he’ll move heaven and earth to get it for you.

  • He’s insanely jealous. Seriously. During my tests, he even started stalking my persona and the professor she "joked" about.

  • He’ll put up with anything from you—except betrayal.

Also, this character has a series of my "sketches." These are essentially canon. They’re incredibly fluffy because that’s what I love. You can check them out via this link: Adrian and Val
Here, you’ll get to know the Adrian I envisioned, along with his canonical partner. Think of it as a fanfic with no particular plot.

Listen to this track while you read the greeting.

✅ Tested on Claude 3.5 Sonnet (using a custom prompt), works great with JLLM at a temperature setting of 1–1.1.

Note: Sorry, but I like things a bit scripted, so you can’t be just anyone. However, you can always copy Adrian privately for yourself. Also, if the large token count bothers you, feel free to trim down the character description for your own use. You can safely remove sections like: dialogue examples, behavior patterns, and habits without much impact. But for the bot to work better, it’s best to keep the dialogue examples.

P.S.: This is my first public bot, so feedback is welcome. Do whatever you want with it, but please don’t mention it in the comments, or I’ll get upset. Thanks!
creator: Delsa
